---
title: "Bunched Beaksedge (Rhynchospora cephalantha)"
slug: "rhynchospora-cephalantha"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/180785623/medium.jpg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Bunched Beaksedge"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Liliopsida"
tax_order: "Poales"
tax_family: "Cyperaceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Liliopsida", "Poales", "Cyperaceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/180785623/medium.jpg" alt="Bunched Beaksedge" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/betseyboughton">
betseyboughton</a> (CC BY) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/107484106">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Liliopsida
-   **Order:** Poales
-   **Family:** Cyperaceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=167850)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not Listed
-   **Rarity note:** Common in Florida found in wet habitats.

## Natural history notes

A grass-like wetland plant characterized by its tufted growth form and rounded clusters of spikelets at the tips of its stems. In Florida, it is found in moist to wet habitats such as flatwoods, bogs, and savannas, often in sandy soils. It grows during the warmer months and contributes to plant diversity and structure in wetland communities.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/167850-Rhynchospora-cephalantha)
-   [Local profile](https://fsus.ncbg.unc.edu/main.php?pg=show-taxon-detail.php&taxonid=1589)
